summary.intent: Enable cookie sharing between multiple browser sessions by reusing a single Chrome instance with multiple tabs instead of launching separate containers per session.
summary.outcome: Refactored the browser pool to share a single browser instance across sessions with the same profile_id using Arc<Browser>, allowing new sessions to open tabs in existing browsers rather than launching new containers.

repo: Each session previously mapped 1:1 to a container/Chrome process/profile lock — the pool tracked pages per browser instance but didn't reuse browsers across sessions.
BrowserInstance struct holds a browser field that needed Arc wrapping to be shared across multiple session handles.
Browser launch functions exist at approximately lines 563 and 742 in pool.rs — two separate code paths for launching browsers.

code.finding: One of two BrowserInstance construction sites where browser field needed to be wrapped in Arc::new()
Second BrowserInstance construction site requiring Arc::new() wrapping for the browser field

workflow: Browser (chromiumoxide or similar) does not implement Clone — shared ownership requires Arc rather than cloning.
When sharing a browser across sessions, the correct CDP approach is creating new Page objects (tabs) rather than new browser processes.

friction: Browser type doesn't implement Clone, requiring a refactor from plain Browser to Arc<Browser> after the initial edit attempt failed to compile.
BrowserInstance construction was split across two separate launch functions (lines 563 and 742), both requiring updates.
open_items: Cleanup/lifecycle management when the last tab for a shared browser instance is closed — the browser process teardown logic may need review.
Isolation between tabs sharing the same profile (cookies are intentionally shared, but other state like navigation history or open modals may interfere between sessions).
